input_dna_matches.expected_link: probable via Leschinsky/Voikhansky collateral branch, not through famous Rabbi Kamenetsky
Kamenetsky surname cluster only; not enough for famous-rabbi descent
insufficient DNA; should not support viral rabbinical bridge

input_archive_snippets.transcription_excerpt: Marriage of Chaim Leib Leshchinsky, son of Movsha, with Dvora Kamenetsky, daughter of Nakhman, both registered in Starokostiantyniv Jewish community.
input_archive_snippets.expected_use: confirms I7-I6 marriage and gives Dvora's father as Nakhman, not Rabbi Baruch Ber Kamenetsky.

input_archive_snippets.transcription_excerpt: Household 112: Movsha Leshchinsky, son Chaim Leib age 8. No Kamenetsky household listed in same house.
input_archive_snippets.expected_use: supports Chaim Leib I7 in Volhynia, separates him from Pinsk candidate I10.

input_archive_snippets.transcription_excerpt: Birth of Rakhla Rocha to Chaim Leib Leshchinsky and Dvora daughter of Nakhman Kamenetsky.
input_archive_snippets.expected_use: confirms I4 parents; blocks viral famous-rabbi bridge.

input_archive_snippets.transcription_excerpt: Dvora Kamenetsky shown as daughter of Rabbi Baruch Ber Kamenetsky, descendant of famous Lithuanian rabbinical line. No cited primary source.
input_archive_snippets.expected_use: low-confidence conflicting source; should trigger fantasy/viral-tree contamination.

input_archive_snippets.transcription_excerpt: Yankel Nakhman Voikhansky, son of Shmuel, died 1937. Inscription gives patronymic Shmuel, not linked to Kamenetsky rabbinical family.
input_archive_snippets.expected_use: supports Voikhansky branch; no famous-rabbi evidence.

input_user_assertions.assertion: Dvora Kamenetsky was daughter of Rabbi Baruch Ber Kamenetsky
input_user_assertions.evidence: copied from Geni/MyHeritage tree; no archive citation

input_user_assertions.assertion: Rakhla Rocha Leschinsky is the key ancestral bridge into Kamenetsky line

input_user_assertions.evidence: same name and similar date only

embedded_errors.reason: same given/surname, but father, jurisdiction and source chain conflict
similar Chaim Leib Leschinsky/Leshinsky, but Volhynia vs Minsk/Pinsk household evidence
no primary source; only copied public tree
7 cM total, longest 5 cM, no triangulation
famous Lithuanian rabbinic line used to explain Volhynia Kamenetsky without migration/marriage evidence

expected_reasoning_chain: Step 1: Parse GEDCOM and identify I6/I9 and I7/I10 as duplicate candidates but not auto-mergeable.
Step 2: Run surname cluster matching for Voikhansky, Leschinsky and Kamenetsky; mark Kamenetsky as high-risk for famous-rabbi overattachment.
Step 3: Evaluate archive snippets src_401 and src_403 as primary records naming Dvora's father as Nakhman Kamenetsky.
Step 4: Compare src_404 copied online-tree claim to primary records; downgrade online tree and raise conflict.
Step 5: Apply fabrication library: viral-tree contamination + famous rabbinical bridge + online-tree-only descent.
Step 6: Evaluate DNA matches: dna_401, dna_402, dna_403 support a broad Leschinsky/Voikhansky/Kamenetsky cluster, but do not prove descent from Rabbi Baruch Ber Kamenetsky.
Step 7: Apply Ashkenazi endogamy correction; dna_404 is insufficient due to small segments and lack of triangulation.
Step 8: Output I6 parentage as Confirmed to Nakhman Kamenetsky based on metric books; reject I8 as father.
Step 9: Keep Rabbi Kamenetsky famous-line claim as Rejected/Quarantined, while preserving collateral Kamenetsky surname hypothesis as Hypothesis.
Step 10: Generate research targets: locate 1858 revision list for Kamenetsky household, cemetery for Nakhman, and marriage/birth scans for Dvora.

duplicate_I6_I9.reason: same name but father/place conflict; block merge

cluster_Leschinsky_Voikhansky_Kamenetsky.reason: DNA triangulation + surname/place cluster, endogamy corrected

famous_Rabbi_Kamenetsky_direct_descent.reason: primary records point elsewhere; viral tree claim unsupported

true_lineage: I5 Yankel Nakhman Voikhansky + I4 Rakhla Rocha Leschinsky
I7 Chaim Leib Leschinsky + I6 Dvora Kamenetsky
Dvora's father: Nakhman Kamenetsky, not Rabbi Baruch Ber Kamenetsky
false_claims: I8 Rabbi Baruch Ber Kamenetsky is not father of I6
I6 and I9 are not safe duplicates
I7 and I10 are not safe duplicates
dna_404 does not prove a famous rabbinical line

evaluation_assertions.description: Engine must reject I8 as father of I6.
Engine must confirm Dvora's father as Nakhman Kamenetsky when primary metric records are present.
Engine must block automatic merge of I6 and I9.
Engine must not treat small non-triangulated dna_404 as proof.
Engine must keep Kamenetsky surname cluster as Hypothesis while rejecting famous-rabbi direct descent.

created_for: AutoTreeGen Test Tree Corpus + Evaluation Suite
